instructions.1: Preheat oven to 350°. Grease a 9' round cake pan
instructions.2: In a saucepan, stir in caramel ingredients and heat over medium heat for 3 minutes. Pour into baking pan.
instructions.3: Cook apples, butter, and salt in a skillet until tender, about 10 minutes. Arrange cooled apples over caramel starting in the center and working out.
instructions.4: In a seperate bowl beat butter and add sugar slowly.
instructions.5: Beat in vanilla and eggs. Add dry ingredients in 3 parts, alternating with milk. Spread batter over apples.
instructions.6: Tap pan twice on counter and bake for 40 minutes. Cool for 20 minutes before running sharp knife around sides and removing cake to a platter. Let cool.

ingredient-list.2: 1/2 cup coconut milk or sour cream

ingredient-list.1: 1 cup seedless cucumber, sliced and quartered
ingredient-list.2: 2 green onions, chopped (use both white and green parts)
ingredient-list.3: 1/4 cup finely diced red bell pepper

instructions.1: Place all sauce ingredients in a blender; blend on medium-high until smooth. Cover and refrigerate sauce until needed.
instructions.2: Cook pasta according to package directions; drain. Transfer cooked pasta to a large bowl, add cold water to cover pasta and let chill approximately 5 minutes or until pasta is no longer warm to the touch.
instructions.3: Drain pasta; add sauce to the bowl and stir to thoroughly coat pasta. (If sauce seems too thick, add 2 tablespoons water and mix well.)
instructions.4: Divide pasta into individual servings and garnish each with cucumber, green onion and red pepper.

Recipes.description: I like using a colorful blend of peppers to brighten this skillet specialty-and a little balsamic vinegar to flavor the tender chicken. If you prefer a stronger flavor, add a little more! Author: Valerie Moore. Stilwell, Kansas.
Recipes.name: Balsamic Chicken and Peppers

ingredients.1: 4 boneless skinless chicken breast halves(4 ounces each)
ingredients.5: 1 each medium sweet yellow red and orange peppers, julienned

instructions.1: Sprinkle chicken with pepper and salt. In a large skillet, cook the chicken in 1 tablespoon oil for 4-6 minutes in each side or until a meat thermometer reach's 170; drain. Remove chicken and keep warm
instructions.2: In the same skillet, sauté peppers and onion in remaining oil for 3-4 minutes or until tender. Return chicken to the pan. Add vinegar; heat through.

ingredient-list.2: 1/2 lb creamy peanut butter chocolate for striping

instructions.1: Melt chocolate in microwave or double broiler. Add other ingredients. Cool until firm. Cut into bars
Preheat oven to 375°. Place all crust ingredients in food processor and pulse; add up to 2 T cold water until dough forms.
instructions.2: Form dough into a disk and wrap in plastic; refrigerate 20 minutes. Press dough into bottom and up sides of a 9' tart pan, line with foil, and add pie weights.
instructions.3: Bake 20 minutes, .move foil and weights, and continue baking 15 minutes.
instructions.4: Whisk first 4 filling ingredients in a saucepan over medium heat until it thickens. Remove from heat; whisk in the rest until creamy.
instructions.5: Pour into cooled crust and refrigerate 2 hours. Toss sugar and berries; let stand 10 minutes and top cheesecake.

Recipes.description: Chock-full or veggies, this aromatic soup has just the right hint of curry. It's a delicious way to use up leftover turkey. - Virginia Anthony. Jacksonville, Florida.
Recipes.name: Curried Turkey Vegetable Soup

ingredients.13: 1 package (9 ounces)fresh baby spinach, coarsely chopped

instructions.1: In a Dutch oven, sauté onions in oil until tender. Stir in flour and curry until blended. Gradually stir in broth. Add the potatoes, celery, carrots, parsley, and sage. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 10-12 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
instructions.2: Stir in the turkey, half-and-half, spinach, salt and pepper. cook and stir until spinach is wilted and soup is heated through.

Recipes.source: Soups and Stews Better homes and gardens win money for recipes (1989)
Recipes.description: Start with a can of potato soup to make this terrific quick- fix vegetable soup - LaNae Minton, Cheyenne, Wyo
Recipes.name: Cheesy Vegetable Soup

ingredients.3: 1 cup frozen loose-pack whole kernel corn
ingredients.7: 1 10 3/4-oz can condensed cream of potato soup

instructions.1: In a Dutch oven, sauté onions in oil until tender. Stir in flour and curry until blended. Gradually stir in broth. Add the potatoes, celery, carrots, parsley, and sage. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 10-12 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
instructions.2: Stir in the turkey, half-and-half, spinach, salt and pepper. cook and stir until spinach is wilted and soup is heated through.

Recipes.description: I invented this fast weeknight dish using frozen tilapia fillets. My entire family loved it! I often serve it with crusty rolls, salad and a rice dish ... and it always gets raves! - CHRISTINE BISSONETTE. SCOTIA, NEW YORK
Recipes.name: Veggie-Topped Tilapia

ingredients.2: 1/3 cup white wine or reduced- sodium chicken broth

instructions.1: Place fillets in a 13-in. x 9-in. baking dish coated with cooking spray. Drizzle with wine or broth; sprinkle with seafood seasoning. Combine the onion, green pepper, tomato, lemon juice, oil and garlic powder; spoon over fillets.
instructions.2: Cover and bake at 425° for 15 minutes. Uncover; sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. Bake 5-10 minutes longer or until vegetables are tender and fish flakes easily with a fork.

Recipes.description:
A piping-hot bowl of this hearty soup can chase away the chills on cold day
Grandma's Secrets
To make this a filling meal, Grandma usually served this soup with fresh bread. For a slightly different twist, serve it in individual bread bowls. To make them, buy small. round loaves of a hearty bread, such as Italian, and slice off a small piece from the top. Then remove the inside of the loaf. leaving a 1A-inch shell; fill with hot soup.
Recipes.name: Chunky Potato Soup

instructions.1: Peel potatoes and cut into 1-inch cubes
instructions.2: Bring water to a boil in large saucepan. Add potatoes and cook until tender. Drain, reserving liquid. Set aside potatoes. Measure 1 cup cooking liquid, adding water, if necessary; set aside.
instructions.3: Peel and finely chop onion. Melt butter in saucepan over medium heat. Add onion to saucepan; cook, stirring frequently, until onion is translucent and tender, but not brown.
instructions.4: Gradually add potatoes, reserved 1 cup cooking liquid, milk and sugar to onion mixture in saucepan; stir well. Add cheese and ham. Simmer over low heat 30 minutes, stirring frequently. Store leftovers, covered, in refrigerator.
